pullRequest.title: [DOM-52765] Handle events with exact same timestamp in execution startup monitoring

nodes.body: no changes to logic in this file, just cleaning up some code and using `ExecutionNodeMonitor.handledEventReasons` like for all the other monitors
i have a comment to that effect on the container monitor one. i can add the same comment to all of them
Just one comment is probably sufficient, anyways either way seems fine to me.
My first guess was that "Pulled" would be the highest priority here, but I guess there's cases where after "Pulled" status, an error can happen; therefore we make the errors as the highest priority?
it's because the same image can be used in multiple containers. the first one can be pulled, then get an error on the second one, so erring toward showing the error.

i mean, there are two instances of "trying pulling" if the same image is used in two containers.
first, it could be pulled.
then the second instance starts and there is some error in the same timestamp. then the error takes precedence.
it's a subtle issue because the second one should just be a "cache hit" event. but i dont know, there might be some error.
I think I agree that based on the edge case you described, this kind of tiebreak order is slightly better.

i also think no matter what order we have, it's not possible to be right in every case -- the fact that k8s events can have the exact same ts prevents knowing (without polling k8s api) _for sure_. unsatisfying, but just have to pick the compromise we think is best
